title: Run-Length Encoding Efficiency for Embedded Sensor Logs
content: Run-length encoding (RLE) represents consecutive identical byte values as (value, count) pairs. For slowly-varying sensor data streams with more than 60% run repetition it achieves 3-8× compression in O(N) time and O(1) space, extending SPI flash page write lifetime. Block CRC32 validation at 4-byte granularity detects storage corruption before data playback.
